title: Praxis SUT Knowledge (Phase 1, active)
description: Active Phase-1 schema. Goal + success/failure signals + risks (with structured triggers) + uncertainties + mandatory provenance/confidence on every assertion (ADR-0004). States and paths from the Phase-1 reference draft stay deferred (ADR-0009): no experiment consumes them in Phase 1 and naming them invites schema rot (docs/06). schema_version stays "0" because the additions are additive: Phase-0 knowledge files validate unchanged.

goal_id.description: Stable id for the goal, reusable across runs/apps (e.g. 'authenticate-user').

goal.description: Human intent as a capability of the system, not a UI action.

type.description: Ordered most->least durable. Raw selectors/xpath/coordinates are intentionally NOT representable. The oracle treats DIFFERENT types as independent evidence; same-type repeats are not independent.

description.description: Human-readable hypothesis: what could break and why.

mitigation.description: Optional: how to prevent / detect the failure when found.

question.description: Open question the agent could not resolve (docs/03). Drives future exploration.

raised_by.description: source_id of the author (agent / human / spec ref).

resolving_signal_value.description: Cross-ref to the signal value that resolved this question.

trigger.description: A risk's trigger: a structured probe a stranger can execute deterministically. Free-text triggers ('under high load', 'race condition') are rejected at write time (ADR-0009 sec 4).

expect.description: Observable response predicate. e.g. 'returns 200 and applied=true'.

action.description: Intent-level action, never a UI selector. e.g. 'submit checkout'.

expect.description: Observable post-condition. e.g. 'same order_id returned both times'.

source_type.description: human/spec = seeded oracle, trusted from cold-start. agent = self-observed, needs evidence diversity AND source-independence to be believed (ADR-0005, ADR-0008).

source_id.description: Agent id, person, or spec reference (e.g. an acceptance-criteria id).

observation_count.description: Times this same signal was seen. Raises confidence WITHIN the signal; does NOT create independence.

auth_state.description: Projected authentication posture for the goal (ADR-0017). Derived from existing success/failure signals via the same diversity-or-seed rule; this is a projection, NOT a new oracle. MUST NOT carry tokens, cookies, user_id, session_id, or any per-session/per-user identifier; the adapter-boundary validator rejects observations that try.

authenticated.description: Whether the current session is believed authenticated, derived from observable behavioral or network signals.

scope.description: Abstract role the projection believes the session occupies. Common values: 'anonymous', 'user', 'admin', or a SUT-specific role string registered in the knowledge file. Null when authenticated is false or surviving evidence is too thin (ADR-0008 diversity-or-seed).

being_tested.description: Whether authentication is the SUBJECT under test (the login flow itself is what the goal verifies) or merely a PRECONDITION (ADR-0027 decision 1). Optional; default false (precondition). When true, a run performs a real login and does NOT reuse a saved session (ADR-0027 decision 2), because reusing a session would skip the flow under test.
